clinical_history: What diagnostic study is most appropriate in this 60-year-old man with rest chest pain?
diagnosis: c) A cardiac catheterization is warranted.  The ECG shows sinus rhythm with ventricular ectopy, left axis deviation, consistent with left anterior fascicular block (hemiblock), and T wave inversions in V2-V5 with subtle upward bowing of the ST segments.  There are also ST-T abnormalities in I and aVL.  The presence of symmetric T wave inversions, especially with upward bowing of the ST segments is highly suggestive of ischemia in the left anterior descending distribution (LAD) in this context.  (Hayden GE, et al. Am J Emerg Med 2002;20:252-262).  This finding is sometimes referred to as the "LAD T wave pattern."  In this patient with a history suggesting unstable angina and these ECG changes, the most expeditious test to order is a cardiac catheterization, which showed significant LAD (and obtuse marginal) disease.
